method: L2 token-type placement analysis of 'div': flanking content-gap classification (word|word enrichment S1, word|num avoidance S2) vs per-inscription gap-reshuffle null (>=500/1000 perm); positive control (synthetic word|word-only detect + uniform false-positive); cross-site (>=15 div) + leave-one-site-out.

positive_control.pc_synthetic_note: Linear B corpus lacks this div structure; PC is fully synthetic (planted word|word-only and uniform-placement corpora built from the real content-token skeletons).

key_findings: Global S1 word|word enrichment: observed 0.749 vs null mean 0.403, perm p=0.0010 (ENRICHED).
Global S2 word|num avoidance: observed count 13 vs null mean 141.747, perm p=0.0010, direction=depleted.
Positive control PASSED: detect word|word-only synthetic (S1 p=0.0020, S2 p=0.0020); uniform-placement false-positive rate=0.067 over 30 draws (synthetic; LB corpus lacks this div structure).
Cross-site: 6 sites with >=15 div; 3 sites significant on BOTH S1 and S2 same-direction; direction_consistent=True; leave-one-site-out survives=True (max LOO p=0.0020).
Flanking structure: before-hist word=382/463; after-hist word=283/463; top transition word>div>word=235.
successor_hypotheses: E058: Does 'div' placement correlate with entry-length (does the divider preferentially separate long word|word pairs vs short ones)? L2 token-count structure.
E059: Is the word|numeral 'bound unit' (the avoided boundary) itself positionally regular — do word+numeral pairs behave as a single counted-entry token in the larger stream grammar?
E060: Cross-token-type: does 'nl' (line break) co-occur with 'div' at entry termini, suggesting a two-level (within-line div, between-line nl) separator hierarchy?
E061: Per-site variation in div density: do sites with fewer divs compensate with longer word runs, or do they use a different separator strategy?
E062: Does the div-avoided word|num boundary align with the E031 word->num order regularity, i.e. is the bound unit directionally constrained (word precedes its numeral)?
E063: Entropy of (before>div>after) transitions vs null — is div placement predictable from local context beyond the word|word/word|num dichotomy?
non_circular: Only token TYPES (word/num/div/nl/other) used; 'div' is the observed token type; no sign values, readings, phonetics, or semantics. Distinct object from E031 (word->num order) and E037 (nl line breaks): this tests the divider's PLACEMENT pattern. Null is calibrated by construction (gap-reshuffle preserves content-token sequence and div count per inscription).
deviations: n_div in the gap-based global analysis = 423, not the raw 463: 40 divs sit at stream start/end with no bracketing content token on one side and are excluded from the gap-based S1/S2 statistics (pre-registered in the gap definition: a div needs both a preceding and following content token to map to a content gap). This is consistent for observed and null (both use the same gap space).
Positive control is fully synthetic (Linear B corpus lacks this div structure, as stated in the task spec); PC corpora were built from the real content-token skeletons with planted div placements.
Leave-one-site-out recomputed global p with 500 permutations per leave-one-out (vs 1000 for the main global); all LOO p-values remained <=0.002.
